signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: Zalgiris Kaunas is a synonym for basketball in Lithuania, a country that lives and breathes the sport. Founded in 1944, it needed just three years to win its first Soviet championship. Four more Soviet titles followed, with triumphs in 1985 through 1987 marking the team’s first golden age. Led by club legend Arvydas Sabonis, Zalgiris lost the 1984 Saporta Cup final to Barcelona and came up short in the 1986 EuroLeague Championship Game against Cibona Zagreb. After Lithuania gained its independence in 1990, Zalgiris won the first nine Lithuanian League championships and also lifted its first-ever European trophy, the 1997-98 Saporta Cup in 1998, downing Adecco Milan in the title game. A year later, Zalgiris shocked the continent by winning the 1999 EuroLeague behind an inspiring performance from Final Four MVP Tyus Edney. In 2004, Zalgiris was one win away from reaching the Final Four, but lost a do-or-die Top 16 finale in overtime against eventual champ Maccabi Tel Aviv. In 2018, Zalgiris returned to the EuroLeague Final Four, where it lost against Fenerbahce Istanbul in the semifinals. Last season, Sylvain Francisco led Zalgiris to its 25th Lithuanian League crown and it also lifted the King Mindaugas Cup. In addition to being the team to beat in Lithuania, Zalgiris now looks for a longer run in this season's EuroLeague.

heroMedia.title: Mathias Lessort returns in style as Panathinaikos downs Zalgiris
heroMedia.summary: The Greens snapped a four-game losing streak

linkedIds.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Zalgiris Kaunas (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Mathias Lessort is finally back! And immediately making a difference.

For the first time since the 2025 Final Four, and nearly 15 months removed from a gruesome leg injury, the former all-EuroLeague center returned to basketball action. In his emotional return, with his family sitting courtside, Lessort was a key cog as Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens snapped a four-game losing skid and downed visiting Zalgiris Kaunas 92-88 on Thursday night.

Cedi Osman scored 24 points on perfect shooting from the field, and Kendrick Nunn netted 22 points. But all eyes were on Lessort, and for all the good reasons.

“He is the heart and soul of this team”, Osman said about his teammate. “We know how much work he put in in this year and a half. We are really happy that he is back. He did a great job today. From the first play, he brought the energy from the beginning and that’s what we were looking for.”

After a lengthy absence, it was not clear how much the big man would be able to contribute. But if this one game was any indication, Lessort will quickly return to being a centerpiece of everything the Greens do.

Lessort started the game, scored after 22 seconds, had a highlight-reel dunk after 90 seconds, and ended up playing 23 minutes and 19 seconds in which he collected 9 points, 3 rebounds, 1 block and a key final-minute assist.

“I am tired, but I am happy”, Lessort told EuroLeague TV interview with his recognizable big smile. “For me, it was the most important to contribute for the team win. It was a crucial moment for us, and we need to start with the wins.”

Not coincidentally, with Lessort the paint, Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens looked a bit more like its old self.

“We moved the ball well, and played more comfortable with Mathias,” Coach Ergin Ataman said.

A quick look at the stats tells that Panathinaikos attempted 50 two-pointers, and scored more than 90 points despite attempting only 11 triples. It also outrebounded Zalgiris 30-23 in the final three quarters.

It's okay to interrupt interviews like that 🫂 pic.twitter.com/lb8OzJIz6F

— EuroLeague (@EuroLeague) March 12, 2026

Ataman stressed that the key was the return of Lessort. And the Panathinaikos coach was so confident in his center’s abilities despite a long absence that he did not shy away from making him a big part of the game plan.

“Zalgiris’s most important player is Moses Wright, and we wanted to get him in foul problems early, we planned to attack him from the beginning with Mathias. Because I saw in practice that he is ready to attack, and we know he can attack very well in the low post,” Ataman explained. “[Lessort] made a great job, and finally after a year and a half, we found our regular game, playing inside-outside basketball game.”

Panathinaikos took the lead for good in the second quarter behind Lessort’s effort and Osman’s baskets. Kostas Sloukas scored 8 consecutive points to end the third quarter, then Nunn and Osman extended the margin to 14 points in the fourth.

However, Sylvain Francisco rallied Zalgiris, which cut it to 85-82 entering the final minute. On the ensuing Panathinaikos possession, the ball went inside to Lessort, who showed no sign of his long absence in game’s key moment, finding Juancho Hernangomez alone on the wing, as the Spaniard made his first basket of the night with 45 seconds to go, sealing the outcome.

Lessort was well aware that he would be the story everyone was be talking about after the game, but he stressed that he wanted to make the night about the team. And he succeeded.

“I tried to execute my routine and approach the game like I approached them before I got injured”, Lessort explained. “Obviously, it was a lot of adrenaline, a lot of rush going through my body. But I tried to calm down, tried to tell myself to approach like I approached the game before. I did not want this game to be about me, I wanted it to be about the team and about the win we need to get. I didn’t try to show something, I didn’t try to be the hero, I was just trying to be a teammate and help my team get a win.”

This win improved Panathinaikos's record to 17-14, tied with Zalgiris. But with seven games to go, the head-to-head sweep Panathinaikos now holds over Zalgiris could play a big role in the final standings. And with Lessort back, and likely only to improve as he gets his legs under him, the Greens have everything to once again become the team to beat.

linkedId.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Zalgiris Kaunas (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Mathias Lessort returns in style as Panathinaikos downs Zalgiris
articleMetadata.description:
Mathias Lessort is finally back! And immediately making the difference.

For the first time since the 2025 Final Four weekend, and nearly 15 months removed from a gruesome leg injury, the former all-EuroLeague center returned to basketball action. In his emotional return, with his family sitting courtside, Lessort was a key cog for Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens snapping a four-game losing skid and downing the visiting Zalgiris Kaunas 92-88 on Thursday night.

heroMedia.title: Valencia survives late Zalgiris rally to stay second
heroMedia.summary: Brancou Badio’s clutch jumper ends a 15-year drought at home against the Lithuanian side

content.text:
For the first time since January 19, 2011, [Valencia Basket](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/valencia-basket/pam/) defeated [Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/zalgiris-kaunas/zal/) on home soil. And it did so the hard way.

When a struggling [Sylvain Francisco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sylvain-francisco/007975/) buried his third three-pointer of the night, tying the game at 87-87 with under a minute remaining, Roig Arena held its breath. The moment felt hauntingly familiar — a painful flashback to Valencia’s recent Copa del Rey semifinal loss to Real Madrid, when Mario Hezonja led a late comeback to stun the hosts.

Zalgiris, which trailed by 12 midway through the fourth quarter, had clawed back with back-to-back threes by [Dustin Sleva](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/dustin-sleva/011927/) and Francisco, resetting the contest at its most critical point.

This time, however, the ending was different. [Brancou Badio](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/brancou-badio/007527/) took command of the next possession, calmly rising for a tough mid-range jumper that restored Valencia’s lead and sent the fans to their feet.

content.title: Valencia Basket-Zalgiris Kaunas | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
“It was a very difficult game,” admitted coach Pedro Martinez afterward. “When they came back and tied the game with Francisco’s three, it was a very tough moment for us mentally, but we played with good personality. The team showed great mentality to suffer; we knew we had to struggle to get this win, so I want to congratulate them for that effort.”

Zalgiris still had a last chance to complete the comeback, but Francisco’s next attempt — another three-pointer with Valencia ahead 89–87 — missed everything, handing possession back to the hosts. [Jean Montero](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jean-montero/010042/) secured the ball and drew a foul to seal the victory at the line.

“I'm very happy with our work,” Martinez added. “Jean Montero was clever at the end, waiting for the foul. And I’d also like to highlight Badio and the shot he made. It’s a good shot for him, but not an easy one. After that, we played very good team defense. Francisco’s shot looked bad, but that’s because of our defense, and I’m happy for that.”

Montero’s two free throws set the final score at 91–87, giving Valencia its fourth straight victory and its 20th win of the EuroLeague season — a milestone never before reached in the club’s history. The win consolidates the team's grip on second place and keeps postseason dreams very much alive in La Fonteta.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Valencia Basket (@valenciabasket)

“I mean, it's exciting,” Omari Moore said, smiling when asked about Valencia’s run. “This is my first year in EuroLeague, but we still have a lot left to go. So, we’re just focusing on it game by game.”

Moore led Valencia in scoring with 16 points and earned special praise from Martinez in the postgame press conference for his composure and leadership down the stretch, especially during Zalgiris’s comeback attempt.

“It definitely was a tough game,” Moore later reflected. “Zalgiris has a really good team. They’ve got one of the MVP frontrunners over there. I thought we did a good job staying composed throughout all 40 minutes, even when they made their runs.”

[Nate Reuvers](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nathan-reuvers/012738/) also stood out, contributing 12 points on 5-for-5 shooting from the field. His performance matched Moore’s PIR of 18, once again showcasing Valencia’s balance and efficiency.

“It’s great. We’re in a great position right now,” Reuvers said. “But as you know, the standings are really tight, and every game it gets even tighter.”

Valencia will now look to extend its winning streak on the road, carrying the confidence of a team that’s learning to close out tough games — and rewriting a bit of history along the way.

articleMetadata.title: Valencia survives late Zalgiris rally to stay second
articleMetadata.description: Valencia Basket held off a furious late comeback by Zalgiris Kaunas to win 91–87 at Roig Arena, earning its first home victory over the Lithuanian club since 2011.

heroMedia.title: Laurynas Birutis sidelined with knee pain
heroMedia.summary: Coach Masiulis revealed ahead of Round 30 that he will miss "a couple of weeks" at least

content.text:
[Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/zalgiris-kaunas/zal/) will be without backup center [Laurynas Birutis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/laurynas-birutis/005504/) for an undisclosed period due to knee pain. 

The 28-year-old has appeared in 29 EuroLeague games this season, averaging 4.6 points and 2.5 rebounds in 10:57 minutes per game while backing up starting center Moses Wright.

Birutis is in his fifth EuroLeague season with Zalgiris, having been with the club since the 2018-19 campaign.

Laurynas Birutis has recently been playing and training while experiencing knee pain, so he will have to spend some time away from basketball. 🚨 pic.twitter.com/id4vGGrSY5

— BC Zalgiris Kaunas (@bczalgiris) March 4, 2026

Head coach Tomas Masiulis acknowledged the impact of the loss ahead of Round 30: "It changes things a lot. He was a very important player, but we have to adapt. One player goes down, another has to step into that role."

On a timeline for Birutis's return, Masiulis remained uncertain: "I don't know. That's more a question for the doctors, but we definitely won't have him for a couple of weeks. Beyond that – we'll see."

articleMetadata.title: Laurynas Birutis sidelined with knee pain
articleMetadata.description: Zalgiris Kaunas will be without backup center Laurynas Birutis for an undisclosed period due to knee pain.

heroMedia.title: Round 29 MVP: Sylvain Francisco, Zalgiris
heroMedia.summary: Sylvain Francisco has earned his second MVP of the Round this season

content.text:
For the second round in a row, the EuroLeague MVP of the Round is a Zalgiris Kaunas player. After Moses Wright's dominance in Round 28, [Sylvain Francisco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sylvain-francisco/007975/) starred in Round 29, leading the Lithuanian champs to a 99-94 double-overtime win over Olympiacos Piraeus.

Francisco racked up 23 points, 3 rebounds, 7 assists, 2 steals, 2 blocks and 8 fouls drawn for a PIR of 36, which was the highest of all players in the round. He made 2 of 3 two-pointers, 4 of 8 three-pointers and 7 of 7 free throws.

This marks Francisco's second MVP of the Round this season after he shared the distinction in Round 7. 

[Elijah Bryant](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/elijah-bryant/009846/) of Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv had the second-highest PIR of the round as he posted a 34 in his team's 92-86 home win over EA7 Emporio Armani Milan. Bryant led the way with 25 points, 5 rebounds, 8 assists and 6 fouls drawn. 

[Talen Horton-Tucker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/talen-horton-tucker/014124/) of Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul and [Jaron Blossomgame](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jaron-blossomgame/011231/) of AS Monaco each amassed PIRs of 33. Horton-Tucker led Fenerbahce to an 81-78 home win over Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade with 29 points, 4 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 steals and 6 fouls drawn. Blossomgame had 25 points on 10-of-10 free-throw shooting plus 7 rebounds in a loss to Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv. [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) of Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens completes the top individual performances in Round 29 with a PIR of 30. In his return to the competition after being chosen the 2025 Final Four MVP with Fenerbahce, Hayes-Davis scored 27 points against Paris Basketball.

### Individual highs: Evan Fournier of Olympiacos, 32 points

Evan Fournier of Olympiacos led all scorers in Round 29 with 32 points against Zalgiris. Nadir Hifi of Paris leads the scorers' table at 19.5 points per game.

Moses Wright of Zalgiris was the only player to register double digits in rebounds, pulling down 13 against Olympiacos. Nikola Milutinov of Olympiacos remains the competition's best rebounder at 7.4 per game.

Nico Laprovittola of FC Barcelona dished 12 assists, which were more than anyone else in Round 29, in his team's road loss against Virtus Bologna. Codi Miller-McIntyre of Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade ranks first overall with 6.9 assists per night.

Luca Vildoza of Virtus collected 5 steals in his team's 85-80 home win over Barca. Aleksa Avramovic of Dubai Basketball leads the league in steals with 1.5 per game.

Wright blocked 4 shots against Olympiacos. Walter Tavares of Real Madrid leads the overall standings at 1.9 blocks per game.

Wright (14 points, 13 rebounds) and Laprovittola (14 points, 12 assists) were the only players to register double-doubles in Round 29.

articleMetadata.title: Round 29 MVP: Sylvain Francisco, Zalgiris
articleMetadata.description: For the second round in a row, the EuroLeague MVP of the Round is a Zalgiris Kaunas player. After Moses Wright's dominance in Round 28, Sylvain Francisco starred in Round 29, leading the Lithuanian champs to a 99-94 double-overtime win over Olympiacos Piraeus.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out
articleMetadata.description: The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

heroMedia.title: Francisco, Williams-Goss light it up from deep as Zalgiris grabs double-overtime win
heroMedia.summary: The two guards stepped up big time and punished Olympiacos when it mattered most

content.text:
History was not in [Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/zalgiris-kaunas/zal/)’s favor heading into the clash against [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/). In fact, the Reds had won the previous nine games between the two teams, with the last victory celebrated by the Lithuanian side coming back in 2021.

Yet, that losing run would end on Wednesday night, as Zalgiris grabbed a 99-94 double-overtime victory against the Greek side. An entertaining dogfight at Zalgirio Arena provided fans with a rollercoaster of emotions as the two teams traded blows from start to finish.

At the end, after 50 minutes of a back-and-forth affair, [Nigel Williams-Goss](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-williams-goss/007982/) and [Sylvain Francisco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sylvain-francisco/007975/) stepped up by firing on all cylinders to down Olympiacos.

content.title: Zalgiris Kaunas-Olympiacos Piraeus | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Despite traveling to Kaunas without one of EuroLeague’s top scorers in [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/) (19.3 PPG) and the league's top rebounder in [Nikola Milutinov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nikola-milutinov/003941/) (7.4 RPG), the visitors delivered a solid outing, with their defense stepping up to hold Zalgiris to 73 points in regulation.

They say that pressure reveals true strength, with Williams-Goss and Francisco proving that when everything was hanging in the balance. The former netted 11 points, with the latter bagging 10 in the 10 minutes of the double overtime as Zalgiris prevailed.

A bucket from [Alec Peters](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/alec-peters/008829/) was followed by a triple from [Evan Fournier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/evan-fournier/013380/) for an 82-87 lead early in the second overtime. But the hosts bounced back with an 8-2 run, as Francisco drilled a triple and Williams-Goss added 5 points to his tally.

[Maodo Lo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/maodo-lo/007032/) hit another three, making it 93-89, but Fournier, who ended the game on a career-high 32 points, trimmed the deficit to a single point by scoring from deep.

Yet, Williams-Goss drilled his fourth triple of the night and split free throws to record a career-high 23 points, leading Zalgiris to victory against his former team.

A victory message from Nigel Williams-Goss himself and a little guest. 🫶🏻 pic.twitter.com/jBAIcXhzLw

— BC Zalgiris Kaunas (@bczalgiris) February 25, 2026

“I’m so proud of my team,” Williams-Goss told EuroLeague TV after the game. “The way we fought and stuck together against, in my opinion, maybe the best team in Europe right now. Just a super, super big-time team victory.”

Francisco also bagged 23 points, with [Moses Wright](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/moses-wright/012878/) dominating the boards and recording a career-high 13 rebounds, which he paired with 14 points.

“To be honest, it was super hard,” Francisco told EuroLeague TV after the game. “It was like a rollercoaster. I think that the team and the staff were really together and trying to find how we were going to win and the way we’re going to punish them and I’m glad we did it. Even if we were down by 5, we came back and got the win.”

This was not just a win for Zalgiris. It was a statement victory against a team that had won seven of their previous eight EuroLeague games heading into Wednesday’s clash. It was also their 10th triumph in 14 duels at Zalgirio Arena, where Tomas Masiulis’s side is a different beast.

What follows might be back-to-back tough clashes away at high-flying Valencia Basket and Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens, but Zalgiris will play five of their last seven games at home, having the opportunity to end the regular season by securing a playoff spot.

And more importantly, with defenses tightening up and games turning into gritty battles down the stretch, in Francisco and Williams-Goss, Zalgiris has two players who can take control when a duel is on the line.

articleMetadata.title: Francisco, Williams-Goss light it up from deep as Zalgiris grabs double-overtime win
articleMetadata.description: Zalgiris Kaunas snapped a nine‑game losing streak against Olympiacos Piraeus with a dramatic 99–94 double‑overtime victory at Zalgirio Arena. Despite missing stars Sasha Vezenkov and Nikola Milutinov, Olympiacos held Zalgiris to 73 points in regulation, but Nigel Williams‑Goss and Sylvain Francisco dominated the extra periods, each scoring 23 points to carry the hosts home.

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

articleMetadata.description: Welcome to this post-domestic cups edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of EuroLeague.net's team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic.

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Zalgiris win Lithuanian Cup for the seventh year running
heroMedia.summary: Moses Wright earned final MVP honors after pouring in 27 points

content.text:
Zalgiris Kaunas lifted Lithuania’s King Mindaugas Cup for the seventh straight year on Sunday by blasting archrival Rytas Vilnius 101-85 in the final at Siauliai Arena.

Moses Wright rocked the paint with 27 points to be named MVP of the final and Sylvain Francisco paired 23 points with 10 assists to lead the winners. Azuolas Tubelis added 14 points on 17 minutes. Ignas Sargiunas paced Rytas with 16 points and Kenneth Smith tallied 15 points and 6 assists in defeat.

Neither team led by more than 4 points in the first quarter, which ended with Zalgiris up 25-21. Francisco drained a pair of threes and Wright dunked twice in a 15-0 run as Zalgiris pulled away to 52-32 in the second quarter. Zalgiris totaled 37 second-quarter points to grab a 62-37 halftime lead.

Rytas came out for the second half to make a push and reeled off 13 straight points – led by Smith and Jerrick Harding – to get within 64-50. Francisco and Wright revived the Zalgiris attack to push the margin back to the 20s and it was 84-64 at the end of the third quarter. Little changed in the fourth quarter as Rytas managed to chip away at the deficit to make the final score more respectable, but the outcome was never in doubt and the Zalgiris faithful started celebrating long before the final buzzer.

articleMetadata.title: Zalgiris win Lithuanian Cup for the seventh year running
